content: Real-time slug uniqueness in Next.js: since Supabase queries must stay server-side (no anon client exposed for stores table per this project RLS design), the standard pattern is a debounced client-side hook (custom useDebounce, ~400ms) that calls a dedicated Server Action (checkSlugAvailability) via useTransition/startTransition on each debounced value change, not a Route Handler+fetch (avoids a second HTTP layer when Server Actions already work in this codebase's established pattern). Validate format with Zod first (cheap, client+server), only hit the DB query if format-valid, to reduce load abandoned by fast typers.
